title: rubus × miscellus
intro: Rubus × miscellus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between the red raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis). The leaves and roots of the thimbleberry plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus × miscellus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health. Rubus × miscellus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbal remedies to enhance its medicinal effects.
